questions.question: Why is health considered more than just the absence of disease?
questions.answer: Because health includes physical, mental, and social well-being.

questions.question: How can mental health affect physical health?
questions.answer: Stress or anxiety can cause headaches, fatigue, and other physical problems.

questions.question: Why is regular exercise important for health?
questions.answer: Exercise strengthens the body, improves immunity, and keeps the body fit.

questions.question: How does pollution affect human health?
questions.answer: Pollution can cause respiratory diseases and other health problems.

questions.question: What is the difference between symptoms and signs?
questions.answer: Symptoms are felt by the patient while signs are observed by doctors.

questions.question: Why are communicable diseases dangerous in crowded areas?
questions.answer: Because pathogens spread quickly through close contact.

questions.question: How can contaminated water spread diseases?
questions.answer: Pathogens present in polluted water enter the body and cause infections.

questions.question: Why are mosquitoes considered vectors?
questions.answer: Because they carry pathogens and transmit diseases to humans.

questions.question: Give one reason for the increase in lifestyle diseases.
questions.answer: Lack of exercise and unhealthy diet.

questions.question: Why is immunity important for the body?
questions.answer: It protects the body from infections and diseases.

questions.question: How do vaccines protect people from diseases?
questions.answer: Vaccines prepare the immune system to recognize and fight pathogens.

questions.question: Why are vaccination programs important for communities?
questions.answer: They reduce the spread of infectious diseases in populations.

questions.question: What is the role of antibiotics in medicine?
questions.answer: Antibiotics treat bacterial infections.

questions.question: Why should antibiotics be used carefully?
questions.answer: Misuse can lead to antibiotic resistance.

questions.question: Why are antibiotics ineffective against viruses?
questions.answer: Antibiotics only kill bacteria and do not affect viruses.

questions.question: How does sanitation help prevent diseases?
questions.answer: Clean surroundings reduce the growth and spread of pathogens.

questions.question: Why should stagnant water be removed from surroundings?
questions.answer: It prevents mosquito breeding.

questions.question: Why is a balanced diet important for immunity?
questions.answer: Nutrients strengthen the immune system.

questions.question: How can stress affect health?
questions.answer: Stress weakens immunity and causes physical health problems.

questions.question: How can hygiene prevent communicable diseases?
questions.answer: Hygiene removes pathogens and reduces infection risk.

questions.question: What is the importance of clean drinking water?
questions.answer: It prevents waterborne diseases.

questions.question: How do community health programs improve public health?
questions.answer: They educate people about hygiene, vaccination, and disease prevention.

questions.question: Why should people complete the full course of antibiotics?
questions.answer: To prevent bacteria from developing resistance.

questions.question: Why are public vaccination campaigns conducted?
questions.answer: To protect large populations from infectious diseases.

questions.question: How can healthy habits prevent diseases?
questions.answer: Healthy habits strengthen the body and improve immunity.
